articles.title: Dollar Rallies on a Hot US PPI Report
articles.expand: The dollar index (DXY00 ) rallied to a 1.5-week high on Wednesday, finishing up by 0.22%. The dollar moved higher on Wednesday on the stronger-than-expected US Apr PPI report, which was hawkish for Fed policy. The dollar also has safe-haven support on concerns that the US-Iran ceasefire may break down...

articles.expand: USD/JPY trades around 157.95 on Thursday at the time of writing, up modestly by 0.05% on the day, as the pair remains close to its highest level in two weeks.

articles.title: Gold holds firm despite hawkish Fed repricing boosts Treasury yields, US Dollar
articles.expand: Gold (XAU/USD) holds firm on Thursday, remaining confined within a week-old trading range as investors closely monitor a high-stakes meeting between US President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping, while US-Iran negotiations remain in limbo.

articles.title: Japanese Yen: Higher yields support prospects for BoJ hike – MUFG
articles.expand: MUFG’s Lee Hardman notes that rising long-term JGB yields, driven by inflation risks linked to the Middle East conflict and hawkish BoJ commentary, are reinforcing expectations for a near-term policy rate increase.

articles.title: US Dollar Threatening to Break Higher
articles.expand: The US dollar is trading quietly against the major currencies. The euro is holding above $1.17 and sterling is holding above $1.35, but the market does not appear done probing these support areas. The greenback has also traded as close to JPY158 as possible without going over. This is where the Bank of Japan may have intervened last week. In the UK...

articles.title: Canadian Dollar: BoC tone tilts mildly hawkish – TD Securities
articles.expand: TD Securities strategists note the Bank of Canada’s (BoC) April Summary of Deliberations balanced US trade risks with inflation concerns.

articles.title: Kazaks: ECB will continue to decide meeting by meeting, based on incoming data
articles.expand: The European Central Bank Governing Council member Martins Kazaks said in a presentation during an event on Thursday, the central bank will continue to decide meeting by meeting and on the basis of incoming data.

articles.title: China new bank loans disappoint in April as sluggish start to the year continues
articles.expand: New yuan loans for the month of April came in at -¥10 billion. Yes, that's another negative reading and one to follow up the supposed blip from July last year. Once is maybe a coincidence, but twice and it's starting to draw questions of whether there is an underlying problem with credit demand conditions. That means the year-to-date figure for tot...

articles.title: Japanese Yen: Sideways versus US Dollar as BoJ turns hawkish – BBH
articles.expand: Brown Brothers Harriman’s (BBH) Elias Haddad notes USD/JPY is trading in a 155.00-160.00 range and expects this to persist until the energy shock fades, despite a constructive stance on Japanese Yen (JPY).

articles.title: US Dollar Index: Supported but capped on topside – OCBC
articles.expand: OCBC strategist Christopher Wong highlights that hot United States (US) Consumer Price Index (CPI) and Producer Price Index (PPI) pushed Treasury yields higher, but US Dollar Index (DXY) gains lacked strong follow-through, suggesting much inflation risk is already priced.

articles.title: Japanese Yen clings to losses against US Dollar as hawkish Fed bets escalate
articles.expand: The Japanese Yen (JPY) reflects weakness against the US Dollar (USD) during the European trading session on Thursday, with the USD/JPY pair holding onto the two-week high near 158.00. The pair remains firm due to the continued outperformance of the US Dollar.

articles.expand: UK economic growth held up better than expected in the first quarter of 2026, with GDP expanding 0.6% qoq in line with forecasts as stronger services activity helped offset softer industrial momentum. articles.expand: Japan's government is considering a supplementary budget to ease household fuel costs ahead of summer, Kyodo reports, sending 30- and 40-year JGB yields higher on increased debt issuance fears. articles.title: ECB’s Lane Lays Out Case for June Hike Amid Global Energy Shock
articles.expand: ECB Chief Economist Philip Lane signaled on Wednesday that the Iran-related oil shock may require ECB tightening to prevent higher energy prices from spreading into wages, inflation expectations, and broader consumer prices. Speaking in London, Lane laid out the intellectual case for a likely June rate hike while acknowledging the Eurozone economy ...

articles.title: BoE’s Mann Warns Bond Market Fragility Matters for Future Rate Hikes
articles.expand: Bank of England policymaker Catherine Mann warned that future interest rate hikes could trigger larger-than-intended tightening in UK financial conditions because of growing fragility in Britain’s gilt market. Speaking on Wednesday, Mann said the increasing role of hedge funds and overseas investors has made the market more sensitive to sudden shif...

articles.title: Govt debt crosses Rs80trn mark by March-end
articles.expand: KARACHI: Pakistan’s central government debt crossed Rs 80 trillion mark by the end of March 2026 mainly driven by higher long-term domestic borrowing. According to the latest data released by the State Bank of Pakistan (SBP) on Wednesday, the country’s total debt stock, comprising both domestic and external liabilities, increased by Rs 2.636 trilli...

articles.title: Deficit down — fault lines remain
articles.expand: The overall fiscal position continues to improve. The government posted an overall budget deficit of 0.7 percent of GDP (Rs856 billion), while the primary surplus stood at 3.2 percent of GDP (Rs4.09 trillion). The primary surplus is the highest, and the fiscal deficit the lowest, in the past thirty years, and perhaps in history. However, this does ...

articles.title: The return of bond vigilantes
articles.expand: Bond markets are beginning to sound like they have finally run out of patience. British long-dated gilt yields recently touched their highest levels since 1998, US Treasury yields remain elevated despite repeated hopes of monetary easing, and investors are once again openly discussing whether governments themselves have become part of the inflation...

articles.title: Rate rise fears surge amid increased budget spending
articles.expand: Economists say increased government spending in the federal budget could prolong inflation and keep interest rates higher for longer. The post Rate rise fears surge amid increased budget spending appeared first on SmartCompany .

articles.title: ECBs Kocher: A June hike is not a baseline for the ECB
articles.expand: ECBs Kocher on the wires saying: A June hike is not a baseline for the ECB Says that the ECB decides on a meeting by meeting basis. A 50 bps hike is very unlikely The market is for a series of hikes going into year-end. This article was written by Greg Michalowski at investinglive.com.

articles.title: Australian Dollar Price Forecast: Minor hurdle comes at 0.7280
articles.expand: The Aussie Dollar continues its march north almost unabated, prompting AUD/USD to confront the area of multi-year peaks. For now, the pair’s positive outlook remains unchanged, propped up by elevated domestic inflation and the RBA’s cautious approach.

articles.title: Australian Dollar Price Forecast: Extra gains still on the cards
articles.expand: The Aussie Dollar continues its march north almost unabated, lifting AUD/USD to the area of multi-year tops. For now, the pair’s positive outlook remains unchanged, propped up by elevated inflation in Oz and the RBA’s hawkish stance.
